summary: A solid Alpine Valley night with Jerry’s soaring vocals and standout Believe It, Blackbird and Pocky Way, captured on a decent audience tape that suffers occasional phase/pitch quirks.
review: The show is widely praised for its emotional highs – Jerry’s voice shines on Morning Dew, the band delivers a fiery Believe It/Or Not and an unforgettable Blackbird>Brokedown, while Brent’s Pocky Way scat steals the spotlight.  The recording is an audience capture that generally sounds good but has noticeable phase‑flip and speed issues, plus a bit of flange on the first set.  Overall the performance outweighs the technical flaws.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers (20+) give 4‑5 stars and highlight strong musical moments; a few note sync or recording glitches, pulling the overall rating slightly below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear enough to enjoy, but suffers from occasional phase‑flip, pitch being off (needs speed correction), and a noticeable flange effect on vocals in the first set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The setlist is praised for its tight song blocks (Masterpiece>Bird Song>Promised Land, Miracle>GSL>Watchtower) and emotional peaks; the first set has a slightly rough edge due to vocal flange, while the second set settles into a smoother, more uplifting groove.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly lauded for powerful vocals and soaring guitar licks; some note his timing slips in one review but overall his voice is described as strong, clear and emotionally resonant.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned positively for big chord work on Dew and occasional rhythmic hiccups; generally regarded as solid.
band_member_comments.Bob: Only a brief query about his role (“What was Weir on?!”); no substantive praise or criticism.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent receives abundant acclaim – “the bomb” vocals, standout scat on Pocky Way, and overall key contributions are highlighted throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming noted for interesting sounds and instrumentation; a few comments about rhythm being slightly out of sync in one negative review.
